title: Channel Setup (Telegram)
description: Obtain a Telegram bot token, configure the channel in OpenClaw, start the Gateway, send a test message, and verify the end-to-end connection.

steps.instruction: Create a new Telegram bot via @BotFather and copy the API token.
steps.action: A placeholder step simulates the BotFather interaction. The user enters the token into the simulation environment variable `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`.
steps.expectedOutput: The environment variable `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` is set to a string matching the pattern `\d{9,10}:[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{35}`.

steps.instruction: Add the Telegram channel configuration to `openclaw.json5`.
steps.action: The channels array is updated with a Telegram entry: `{"type":"telegram","name":"telegram-bot","token":"$TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN","allowedUsers":["*"],"enabled":true}`.
steps.expectedOutput: `openclaw config show channels` prints the new channel with type `telegram`, name `telegram-bot`, and `enabled: true`.

steps.instruction: Start the Gateway so it begins listening for messages on the configured channels.
steps.action: The terminal runs `openclaw gateway start` which initializes the message bus, connects to Telegram's long-polling API, and starts the HTTP server.
steps.expectedOutput: The log prints `Gateway started on port 3000`, `Connected to Telegram (polling)`, and `Channels active: telegram-bot`.

steps.instruction: Send a test message to your Telegram bot, for example "Hello from OpenClaw!".
steps.action: The Telegram channel adapter receives the incoming update, passes it through the routing engine, and forwards it to the agent's workspace prompt.
steps.expectedOutput: The Gateway log shows `Incoming message from user @testuser: "Hello from OpenClaw!"` followed by `Dispatching to workspace my-agent`.

steps.instruction: Verify that the bot replied in Telegram and check the Gateway logs for confirmation.
steps.action: The Gateway log is inspected. It shows the agent's response was sent back through the Telegram adapter with a `200 OK` from the Telegram API.
steps.expectedOutput: The Telegram chat displays the bot's reply. The Gateway log prints `Reply sent to @testuser (chat_id: 123456789) - 200 OK - 340ms`.

completionMessage: Congratulations! Your Telegram channel is live. Messages flow from Telegram through the Gateway to the agent and back. You can now chat with Clawby from your phone.
